div.h1: Why chroot() is NOT Container Isolation
div.p: The Classic Escape via Directory Traversal

g.text: No path to host after umount

ul.li: Directory entries on disk still point to real parent
.. from /jail/escape still resolves to /jail on disk
Detaches old root from mount hierarchy
After umount(), no kernel data structure links to host

div.p: chdir("..") from /jail/escape ↓ (follows disk directory entry) Reaches /jail (outside chroot!) ↓ chdir("..") repeatedly → reaches / ↓ chroot(".") → full host root

ul.li: Old root can be completely unmounted
Combined with mount namespace = true isolation
